# flashduty_alert_pipeline (Data Source)

Use this data source to get information about alert processing pipeline rules for a Flashduty integration.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up alert pipeline rules for an integration
data "flashduty_alert_pipeline" "example" {
  integration_id = 5592304204454
}

output "pipeline_rules" {
  value = data.flashduty_alert_pipeline.example.rules
}

output "rule_count" {
  value = length(data.flashduty_alert_pipeline.example.rules)
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `integration_id` (Number) The ID of the integration.

### Read-Only

- `rules` (Attributes List) Ordered list of pipeline rules. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules))

<a id="nestedatt--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules`

Read-Only:

- `if` (Attributes List) Filter conditions (AND logic). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--if))
- `kind` (String) The rule kind: `title_reset`, `description_reset`, `severity_reset`, `alert_drop`, or `alert_inhibit`.
- `settings` (String) JSON-encoded settings for the rule.

<a id="nestedatt--rules--if"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.if`

Read-Only:

- `key` (String) The attribute or label key.
- `oper` (String) The operator: `IN` or `NOTIN`.
- `vals` (List of String) The values to match.
